userManagement.enforcementOn: Workspace enforcement is ON — this user cannot choose their own model.
userManagement.resolutionOrder: Resolution: Enforcement → User override → Workspace default → Auto-select
userManagement.selectProviderHint: Select a provider to load its models.
userManagement.cannotChangeOwnRole: Cannot change your own role in bulk edit
userManagement.addsToEachUserCredits: Adds to each user's existing credits
userManagement.bulkQuotaNote: These amounts are added to each selected user's current totals (not replaced).
userManagement.cannotChangeOwnRoleBulk: You're in the selection. Deselect yourself before changing roles in bulk — you can't change your own platform role.

planLimits.description: Configure limits for each plan tier. Changes take effect immediately for all users on that plan. Set values to override defaults, or reset to use hardcoded defaults.

page.title: System Administration
page.subtitle: Manage platform features, users, AI tools, and access controls
page.linkProjectsTitle: All projects (drafts + published) with framework, owner, chat activity
page.linkRuntimeTitle: Published apps + live dev servers — CPU, memory, uptime, controls
page.linkChatTitle: All AI chat sessions (redacted, audit-logged) — for training & abuse review
page.featuresHint: Toggle features on/off globally. Set minimum plan or workspace role requirements.

adminComponents.bothConfigsSaved: Both configurations are saved. The selected tab is what this user will use.
adminComponents.saveResetUsageTitle: Save credits and reset daily/monthly usage to 0

thumbnails.description: Generate missing project thumbnails and view the generation log.
thumbnails.empty: No thumbnail generation logs yet. Click "Generate Missing Thumbnails" to start.

copilotSessions.autoRefreshHint: Auto-refreshes every 5 seconds. Each engine is a per-project Copilot CLI subprocess.
copilotSessions.noEngines: No active Copilot engines. Sessions start when users interact with AI in the editor.

signups.confirmBlock: Block {email}? Their account will be denied AND the email will be permanently prevented from signing up again, even if you later delete the user.
signups.confirmDeny: Deny {email}? They will not be able to log in.
signups.confirmUnblock: Unblock {email}? They will be able to sign up again.
signups.settingsDescription: When on, new signups go to a pending queue and cannot log in until you approve them. Existing accounts are unaffected.
signups.pendingMessageHint: Shown on the signup screen after a new user submits, and on the login screen if they try to sign in while pending.
signups.pendingEmpty: No one is waiting for approval right now.
signups.recentlyDeniedDescription: These users were denied. They can't log in. Approve to grant access.
signups.blockedDescription: These emails can never sign up — not even via GitHub or Google. Unblock to allow signup again.

mfa.introDescription: MFA is optional and user-controlled. Use the reset action only when a user has lost access to both their authenticator app and recovery codes.
mfa.empty: No users have enabled MFA yet.
mfa.dialogDescription: This will remove all MFA factors and recovery codes for this user, and sign them out of every active session. They will be able to sign in with just their password until they enroll again.

frameworks.minOneEnabled: At least one framework must remain enabled
frameworks.title: Project Frameworks
frameworks.description: Control which project types users can create. Disabled frameworks won't appear in the project creation dialog.
frameworks.footer: Changes take effect immediately. Existing projects using a disabled framework will continue to work, but new projects cannot be created with it.

embedding.validationRequired: Provider, base URL, model and API key are all required.
embedding.title: Platform Embedding Model
embedding.description: Set once — every workspace inherits this for chatbots, semantic search and RAG. End users of generated apps never see this UI; they just prompt the AI to build a chatbot.
embedding.notConfigured: Not configured — calls to /__doable/ai/embed will return EMBEDDING_NOT_CONFIGURED
embedding.presetCustomDesc: Any /v1/embeddings endpoint — Ollama, vLLM, Voyage proxy, etc.
embedding.apiKeyPlaceholderKeep: Leave blank to keep current key (replace any to update)
embedding.saveHint: Validates with a probe call before persisting.

planDefaults.applyFailed: Failed to apply defaults to existing workspaces
planDefaults.description: Set the default AI model and provider for each plan tier. New workspaces will automatically inherit these settings.
planDefaults.applyToExistingTitle: Apply this default to all existing workspaces on this plan
planDefaults.noCopilotAccounts: No Copilot accounts found. Add one in AI Settings first.
planDefaults.noCustomProviders: No custom providers found. Add one in AI Settings first.
planDefaults.noneExplanation: No default AI model will be configured for new {plan} workspaces. Users will need to set up their own AI connection.
planDefaults.applySkipped: {count} workspace(s) skipped — already had AI configured. Use "overwrite" to force-apply.
planDefaults.overwriteLabel: Overwrite existing workspace AI settings when applying to existing
planDefaults.howItWorks1: When a new user signs up, their workspace inherits the default for their plan tier.
planDefaults.howItWorks2: The Copilot account or provider is cloned into the user's workspace automatically.
planDefaults.howItWorks3: Users can override the default in their own AI Settings.
planDefaults.howItWorks4: Use "Apply to Existing" to retroactively push defaults to workspaces that don't have AI configured yet.

toolsConfig.help: Configure which tools are available in each AI mode. Strategize (plan mode) typically uses read-only tools for analysis. Build mode has full file creation and editing tools. Changes take effect on new sessions within 60 seconds.
toolsConfig.confirmDelete: Delete the "{mode}" mode configuration? This will cause the mode to use hardcoded defaults.
toolsConfig.emptyTools: No tools configured — will use hardcoded defaults
toolsConfig.planWriteWarning: Strategize mode with write tools enabled may cause unintended file modifications.

email.saved: Email provider saved! Send a test email to verify.
email.testSent: Test email sent! Check your inbox.
email.confirmDelete: Remove email configuration? The system will fall back to environment variable settings.
email.deleted: Configuration removed. Falling back to environment variables.
email.smtpDesc: Gmail, SendGrid, Mailgun, SES, and 20+ providers
email.resendDesc: Modern email API with great deliverability
email.gmailOAuthHint: Connect your Google account with one click. This uses OAuth so your password is never shared. Only the gmail.send permission is requested.
email.label: Label
email.noConfig: No email provider configured in the database
email.confirmRetryAll: Retry all {status} emails? They will be reset to pending.
email.confirmPurge: Permanently delete ALL {label} emails? This cannot be undone.
email.infoFooter: All credentials are encrypted at rest using AES-256. The email queue retries failed sends with exponential backoff (up to 5 attempts). After exhausting retries, emails move to the dead-letter queue for manual review.

dns.title: DNS for Published Sites
dns.subtitle: Each publish either gets its own Cloudflare CNAME, or rides on a wildcard CNAME we set up for you.
dns.acmUndetectableTitle: The cloudflared OAuth token has DNS:Edit scope but not SSL/Certificates:Read — we can't see ACM packs. If you have ACM, tick the override below to bypass detection.
dns.diagnosticsFailed: Could not load Cloudflare zone diagnostics: {error}
dns.perPublishDesc: Every publish calls the Cloudflare API to create one CNAME per subdomain. Lets multiple doable servers coexist under the same zone using <env>-<slug>.{zone} hostnames covered by Universal SSL. Requires CF_API_TOKEN, CF_ZONE_ID, and CLOUDFLARED_TUNNEL_ID.
dns.wildcardDesc: Skip the per-publish API call — all publishes resolve via one wildcard CNAME pointing to your tunnel. We'll create the record on your zone with the Cloudflare token from cloudflared tunnel login.
dns.freeApexDetail: works out of the box (Universal SSL).
dns.paidAcmDetail: pick any *.<anything>.{zone} and tick the override below if our token can't see your ACM packs.
dns.acmOverrideLabel: I have Advanced Certificate Manager on this zone. Auto-detection can't read ACM status with the tunnel OAuth token, so tick this if you've already enabled ACM and want to create a multi-level wildcard.
dns.noWildcardWarning: No wildcard CNAME currently active on this zone. Publishes will fail until you auto-configure one above, or switch back to per-publish mode.
dns.deleteConfirm: Delete {hostname}? Publishes pointing to it stop resolving.
dns.kekMismatch: KEK mismatch. The stored token can't be decrypted under the current DOABLE_KEK (likely a backup restore with a stale .env). The cert.pem fallback is keeping DNS ops working, but ACM detection is degraded. Re-paste the token below, or click Remove override to drop the stale row.
dns.tokenOptionalHint: Strictly optional. DNS auto-configure, wildcard create/delete, and per-publish CNAMEs all work fine with the cert.pem token from cloudflared tunnel login alone. Adding a custom token only unlocks accurate ACM detection so the badge above can flip from ACM status unknown to ACM enabled or No ACM.
dns.tokenPermissionsHint: → Create Token → Create Custom Token. Add exactly these zone permissions:
dns.scopeHint: Scope to your specific zone ({zone}) under "Zone Resources". Then paste the token here.
dns.headsUp: Heads up: A *.{zone} CNAME can point to exactly one tunnel — running multiple doable servers on the same zone requires Cloudflare Advanced Certificate Manager so each environment can have its own multi-level wildcard (e.g. *.staging.{zone} and *.prod.{zone}).
dns.noSettingYet: No setting persisted yet — using the per-publish default. Selecting an option or auto-configuring wildcard will save it for future publishes.
dns.hostnameFormat: Wildcard must be lowercase a-z, 0-9, dots and hyphens (e.g. *.server.example.com).
dns.hostnameInZone: Wildcard must be inside zone {zone} (e.g. *.{zone} or *.<sub>.{zone}).

trace.searchDescription: Search traces across services. Click a row to open the flame graph.
trace.clickSpanHint: Click a span in the flame graph to inspect.

audit.title: Prompt & conversation audit
audit.description: Search every AI conversation on the platform. Every search and view you perform is recorded for compliance.
audit.adminActionLogTitle: History of admin actions taken on the audit surface
audit.notEnabled: Audit log is not yet enabled on this platform
audit.noResults: No conversations match the current filters.
audit.actionsDescription: Every search and view performed on the audit surface, with viewer identity, timestamps, and request metadata.
audit.noMessages: This session has no recorded messages.

moderation.title: Marketplace moderation
moderation.description: Review listings flagged for moderator approval and respond to community reports.
moderation.decisionNotePlaceholder: Optional decision note (sent to publisher on rejection)…

runtime.accessDescription: This page shows runtime state for every project on the host.
runtime.title: Runtime
runtime.description: Published apps & live editor dev servers — CPU, memory, uptime, controls.
runtime.confirmStop:
Stop "{projectName}"?

The app's systemd unit will be terminated. The user can restart it from their editor.
runtime.confirmKillDevServer:
Kill dev server for "{projectName}"?

The Vite process will be terminated. The user can restart it from their editor.
runtime.devServersHint: Vite/Next dev servers spawn when a user opens the editor preview, and exit when the API restarts or the user idles out.
runtime.devServersFooter: Source: in-memory servers map in dev-server-core.ts. Memory is RSS from /proc/<pid>/status. Ports allocated from 3100-3200.
runtime.filterPlaceholder: Filter by project, owner, framework, port…
runtime.publishedHint: Runtime rows appear here when a user clicks Publish. To see ALL projects (including drafts), open Projects.
runtime.stopTitle: systemctl stop — terminate the running process
runtime.publishedFooter: Metrics source: systemd cgroups via /sys/fs/cgroup/system.slice/doable-app@<slug>.service. Sandbox user is the per-project Linux UID created by setupProjectUser().
runtime.allowListEmpty: None configured. App can reach localhost only — all other outbound TCP is blocked by IPAddressDeny=any.
runtime.denialsEmpty: No deny events. (No outbound attempts blocked, or systemd's BPF firewall isn't logging at this level.)
runtime.egressFooter: Systemd unit: {unit}. Egress policy is enforced at the cgroup level by systemd's IPAddressDeny=any + per-project IPAddressAllow=....
runtime.redactedBanner: Secrets auto-redacted (passwords, JWTs, API keys, hex blobs, DB URLs). Every view is recorded in the admin audit log.

projects.title: All Projects ({total})
projects.description: Every project on the platform — drafts, published, and live runtime — with framework, owner, chat activity.
projects.searchPlaceholder: Filter by name, slug, owner email, framework, workspace…
projects.chatLinkTitle: View chat sessions for this project

chat.title: Chat Sessions ({total})
chat.description: Every AI conversation across the platform — for training, audit, abuse review.
chat.privacyBody: Message content is auto-redacted (passwords, JWTs, API keys, hex blobs, DB URLs). Every thread you open is recorded in the admin audit log with your name + timestamp.
chat.searchPlaceholder: Filter by project name or user email…
chat.empty: No chat sessions match your filter.
